ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR CLAIMANTS WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER AND GREENSTEIN & MCDONALD'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONTINUE DATE FOR HEARING ON FINAL ACCOUNTING AND DISCHARGE OF RECEIVER; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO MODIFY THE COURT'S ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER HEARING SET FOR AUG-10-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER APPROVING APPLICATION OF RECEIVER FOR INSTRUCTIONS ON AND MODIFICATIONS TO ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER

LAW AND MOTION 302, CLAIMANTS WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER AND GREENSTEIN & MCDONALD'S MOTION TO CONTINUE DATE FOR HEARING ON FINAL ACCOUNTING AND DISCHARGE OF RECEIVER; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO MODIFY THE COURT'S ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: RECEIVER'S REQUEST FOR AN ORDER SETTLING AND APPROVING THE FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT AND DISCHARGING THE RECEIVER IN ALL CASES EXCEPT #CGC-09-490868 IS GRANTED. THE RECEIVER IS ALSO PERMITTED TO HOLDBACK $50,600 ($50,000 IN LEGAL FEES AND $600 IN BANK FEES) IN CASE #CGC-09-490868 TO COVER FUTURE EXPENSES. THE REQUEST THAT THE COURT NOT DISCHARGE THE RECEIVER IN ANY OF THE RELATED RECEIVERSHIPS UNTIL SUCH TIME THAT THE COURT IS PREPARED TO DISCHARGE THE RECEIVER IN ALL OF THE RELATED RECEIVERSHIPS IS DENIED. CLAIMANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE THE HEARING IS DENIED. THE RECEIVER'S REQUEST FOR A HOLDBACK OF $5,600 IN EACH CASE OTHER THAN #CGC-09-490868 IS DENIED. THE RECEIVER IS TO RECOVER HIS INCURRED FEES FROM THE RECEIVERSHIP ESTATES. FURTHER, THE COURT INDICATED THAT IN THE REMAINING RECEIVERSHIP (#CGC-09-490868), THE RECEIVER IS TO BE PAID PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER. RECEIVER TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT INDICATES EXACTLY WHAT FUNDS ARE BEING DISPERSED FROM RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE. THE COURT SIGNED AN ORDER RE: THE JUNE 30, 2011 HEARING ON CASE #CGC-09-490868.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RECEIVER TODD ROBINETTE'S APPLICATION FOR INSTRUCTIONS ON AND MODIFICATIONS TO ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER - THE COURT TOOK UNDER SUBMISSION THAT PORTION RE LARAMAR ENTITLED TO FEES. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FINAL - RECEIVER'S APPLICATION FOR MODIFICATIONS TO ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE REQUEST TO CHANGE THE FEE STRUCTURE IS DENIED. THE REQUEST TO BE ENTITLED TO FEES UP TO THE POINT THE MOTION TO DISMISS IS FILED IN THE BESS CASE IS GRANTED. IF THE BESS CASE IS NOT DISMISSED, THE RECEIVER MAY REQUEST FURTHER FEES. THE COURT GRANTS THE ORDER AUTHORIZING THE RECEIVER TO OBTAIN COVERAGE COUNSEL UP TO THE POINT OF THE DECISION ON THE MOTION TO DISMISS. TENANTS REQUEST FOR FEES TO BE SET ASIDE FOR RECOVERY IN THE BESS CASE IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS AND CONFIRMS THAT LARAMAR IS ENTITLED TO FEES UP TO JUNE 3, 2011 PURSUANT TO THE AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE RECEIVER AND LARAMAR FOR FINAL ACCOUNTING. RECEIVER TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, TODD ROBINETTE'S NOTICE OF HEARING RE: RECEIVER'S FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: RECEIVER'S REQUEST FOR AN ORDER SETTLING AND APPROVING THE FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT AND DISCHARGING THE RECEIVER IN ALL CASES EXCEPT #CGC-09-490868 IS GRANTED. THE RECEIVER IS ALSO PERMITTED TO HOLDBACK $50,600 ($50,000 IN LEGAL FEES AND $600 IN BANK FEES) IN CASE #CGC-09-490868 TO COVER FUTURE EXPENSES. THE REQUEST THAT THE COURT NOT DISCHARGE THE RECEIVER IN ANY OF THE RELATED RECEIVERSHIPS UNTIL SUCH TIME THAT THE COURT IS PREPARED TO DISCHARGE THE RECEIVER IN ALL OF THE RELATED RECEIVERSHIPS IS DENIED. CLAIMANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE THE HEARING IS DENIED. THE RECEIVER'S REQUEST FOR A HOLDBACK OF $5,600 IN EACH CASE OTHER THAN #CGC-09-490868 IS DENIED. THE RECEIVER IS TO RECOVER HIS INCURRED FEES FROM THE RECEIVERSHIP ESTATES. FURTHER, THE COURT INDICATED THAT IN THE REMAINING RECEIVERSHIP (#CGC-09-490868), THE RECEIVER IS TO BE PAID PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER. RECEIVER TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT INDICATES EXACTLY WHAT FUNDS ARE BEING DISPERSED FROM RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE. THE COURT SIGNED AN ORDER RE: THE JUNE 30, 2011 HEARING ON CASE #CGC-09-490868.

Claimants Wartelle, Weaver & Schreiber And Greenstein & Mcdonald'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Date For Hearing On Final Accounting And Discharge Of Receiver; Or In The Alternative, To Modify The Court'S Order Appointing Receiver
Off Calendar Aug-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CLAIMANTS WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER AND GREENSTEIN & MCDONALD'S MOTION TO CONTINUE DATE FOR HEARING ON FINAL ACCOUNTING AND DISCHARGE OF RECEIVER; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO MODIFY THE COURT'S ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER.
Appearances: Mark R. Mengelberg and Lawrence Butler of Seyfarth Shaw LLP (397.2823) for Receiver and moving party; Eugene Chang and Julie Fieber of Stein & Lubin LLP (981.0550) for plaintiff Veritas-B Mezz B7, LLC; J. Scott Weaver of West Bay Law Wartelle Weaver & Schreiber (693.0504) for claimants on case #CGC-09-490868 Wartelle Weaver & Schreiber and Greenstein & McDonald
Argued and the court ruled as follows: Receiver's Request for an Order Settling and Approving the Final Account and Report and Discharging the Receiver in all cases except #CGC-09-490868 is granted. The Receiver is also permitted to holdback $50,600 ($50,000 in legal fees and $600 in bank fees) in case #CGC-09-490868 to cover future expenses. The Request that the Court not discharge the Receiver in any of the related receiverships until such time that the Court is prepared to discharge the Receiver in all of the related receiverships is denied. Claimants' Motion to Continue the Hearing is denied. The Receiver's request for a holdback of $5,600 in each case other than #CGC-09-490868 is denied. The Receiver is to recover his incurred fees from the receivership estates. Further, the court indicated that in the remaining receivership (#CGC-09-490868), the receiver is to be paid pursuant to the court's prior order. Receiver to submit a proposed order that indicates exactly what funds are being dispersed from receivership estate. The court signed an order re: the June 30, 2011 hearing on case #CGC-09-490868. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Application And Applicaiton For Instructions On And Modifications To Order Appointing Receiver
Off Calendar Jun-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RECEIVER TODD ROBINETTE'S APPLICATION FOR INSTRUCTIONS ON AND MODIFICATIONS TO ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER.

APPEARANCES: Julie L. Fieber, Esq. of Stein & Lubin LLP 955-5091 for pltf & opposing party; Lawrence E. Butler, Esq. 544-1059 & Mark R. Mengelberg, Esq. 544-1013 of Seyfarth Shaw LLP for receiver & moving party; J. Scott Weaver, Esq. of Wartelle, Weaver & Schreiber 693-0504 for "Bess pltfs".

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling, however took under submission that portion re Laramar entitled to fees. After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted as final - RECEIVER'S APPLICATION FOR MODIFICATIONS TO ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE REQUEST TO CHANGE THE FEE STRUCTURE IS DENIED. THE REQUEST TO BE ENTITLED TO FEES UP TO THE POINT THE MOTION TO DISMISS IS FILED IN THE BESS CASE IS GRANTED. IF THE BESS CASE IS NOT DISMISSED, THE RECEIVER MAY REQUEST FURTHER FEES. THE COURT GRANTS THE ORDER AUTHORIZING THE RECEIVER TO OBTAIN COVERAGE COUNSEL UP TO THE POINT OF THE DECISION ON THE MOTION TO DISMISS. TENANTS REQUEST FOR FEES TO BE SET ASIDE FOR RECOVERY IN THE BESS CASE IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS AND CONFIRMS THAT LARAMAR IS ENTITLED TO FEES UP TO JUNE 3, 2011 PURSUANT TO THE AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE RECEIVER AND LARAMAR FOR FINAL ACCOUNTING. Receiver to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
